Course Title: AI in Immigration and Border Processing
Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Immigration officers, border control agents, policy analysts, government administrators, public sector managers, civil servants, case workers, compliance officers
Team or Dept this is for: Immigration Services, Border Security, Government Operations, Policy Development, Public Administration, Legal Affairs, Compliance
Description: Risks in classification, profiling, and decision support systems.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how immigration or border control processes work, along with a general interest in how technology is used in public services, is all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with AI-driven classification, profiling, and decision support systems used in immigration and border processing contexts. You will also be able to explain how these risks affect individuals and communities, and apply basic frameworks to evaluate the fairness and accountability of AI tools in public sector environments.
